title: Johnston, Rhode Island

infobox.value: Linda Folcarelli (D) Lauren Garzone (D) Alfred T. Carnevale (D) Robert V. Russo (D) Robert J. Civetti (D)

is a [town](/wiki/New_England_town "New England town") in [Providence County](/wiki/Providence_County,_Rhode_Island "Providence County, Rhode Island"), [Rhode Island](/wiki/Rhode_Island "Rhode Island"), United States. The population was 29,568 at the [2020 census](/wiki/United_States_Census,_2020 "United States Census, 2020"). Johnston is the site of the [Clemence Irons House](/wiki/Clemence_Irons_House "Clemence Irons House") (1691), a [stone-ender](/wiki/Stone-ender "Stone-ender") museum,[[3]](#cite_note-3) and the [only landfill](/wiki/Central_Landfill "Central Landfill") in Rhode Island. Incorporated on March 6, 1759, Johnston was named for the colonial attorney general, [Augustus Johnston](/wiki/Augustus_Johnston "Augustus Johnston").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-johnstonhistorical.org-5)

**Johnston** is a [town](/wiki/New_England_town "New England town") in [Providence County](/wiki/Providence_County,_Rhode_Island "Providence County, Rhode Island"), [Rhode Island](/wiki/Rhode_Island "Rhode Island"), United States. The population was 29,568 at the [2020 census](/wiki/United_States_Census,_2020 "United States Census, 2020"). Johnston is the site of the [Clemence Irons House](/wiki/Clemence_Irons_House "Clemence Irons House") (1691), a [stone-ender](/wiki/Stone-ender "Stone-ender") museum,[[3]](#cite_note-3) and the [only landfill](/wiki/Central_Landfill "Central Landfill") in Rhode Island. Incorporated on March 6, 1759, Johnston was named for the colonial attorney general, [Augustus Johnston](/wiki/Augustus_Johnston "Augustus Johnston").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-johnstonhistorical.org-5)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnston,_Rhode_Island&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the town has a total area of 24.4 square miles (63 km2), of which 23.7 square miles (61 km2) is land and 0.7 square miles (1.8 km2) (2.91%) is water.

### Neighborhoods

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnston,_Rhode_Island&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Neighborhoods")]

Neighborhoods in Johnston:
[Winsor Hill](/w/index.php?title=Winsor_Hill&action=edit&redlink=1 "Winsor Hill (page does not exist)"),
[Thornton](/wiki/Thornton,_Rhode_Island "Thornton, Rhode Island") (includes part of [Cranston](/wiki/Cranston,_Rhode_Island "Cranston, Rhode Island")),
[Graniteville](/wiki/Graniteville,_Rhode_Island "Graniteville, Rhode Island"),
[Hughesdale](/wiki/Hughesdale,_Rhode_Island "Hughesdale, Rhode Island"),
[Morgan Mills](/w/index.php?title=Morgan_Mills,_Rhode_Island&action=edit&redlink=1 "Morgan Mills, Rhode Island (page does not exist)"),
[Manton](/wiki/Manton,_Rhode_Island "Manton, Rhode Island"),
[Simmonsville](/w/index.php?title=Simmonsville,_Rhode_Island&action=edit&redlink=1 "Simmonsville, Rhode Island (page does not exist)"),
[Pocasset](/w/index.php?title=Pocasset,_Johnston,_Rhode_Island&action=edit&redlink=1 "Pocasset, Johnston, Rhode Island (page does not exist)"),
[West End](/w/index.php?title=West_End,_Rhode_Island&action=edit&redlink=1 "West End, Rhode Island (page does not exist)"),
[Belknap](/wiki/Belknap,_Rhode_Island "Belknap, Rhode Island"), Moswansicut Lake, Merino and
[Frog City](/wiki/Frog_City,_Rhode_Island "Frog City, Rhode Island").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnston,_Rhode_Island&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: History")]

The area was first settled by [English](/wiki/England "England") settlers in the seventeenth century as a farming community. In 1759 the town officially separated from Providence and was incorporated on March 6, 1759. Johnston was named for the current colonial attorney general, [Augustus Johnston](/wiki/Augustus_Johnston "Augustus Johnston"), who was later burned in [effigy](/wiki/Effigy "Effigy") during the [Stamp Act](/wiki/Stamp_Act "Stamp Act") protests in 1765 and then fled Rhode Island as a Tory during the American Revolution in 1779.[[5]](#cite_note-johnstonhistorical.org-5) The first house of worship in Johnston opened when the Baptist Meeting House in Belknap was constructed in 1771. During the American Revolution Rhode Island's only gunpowder mill was constructed in Graniteville, and the town hosted American General [John Sullivan](/wiki/John_Sullivan_(general) "John Sullivan (general)") for a dinner in 1779 upon his departure from Rhode Island to fight in New York. In 1790 the [Belknap School](/wiki/Belknap_School "Belknap School"), the first public school in the town, was founded. In 1791 the Providence and Norwich Turnpike (today's Plainfield Pike) was chartered.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

At the 2020 [census](/wiki/Census "Census"), there were 29,568 people and 11,359 households in the town. The population density was 1,259.4 inhabitants per square mile (486.3/km2). There were 12,501 housing units in the town. The racial makeup of the town was 79.45% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 3.46% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 0.34% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 3.05% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.06% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander_(U.S._Census) "Pacific Islander (U.S. Census)"), 6.52% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), and 7.12% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 13% of the population.

There were 11,359 households, of which 28.6% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 44.7% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 30.5% had a female householder with no spouse present, and 16.7% had a male householder with no spouse present. 11.7% of all households were made up of individuals, and 5.1% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.54 and the average family size was 3.09.

Age distribution was 19.3% under the age of 18, 6.6% from 18 to 24, 26.4% from 25 to 44, 26.4% from 45 to 64, and 21.2% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 43 years.

The [median household income](/wiki/Median_household_income "Median household income") was $87,514, and the median family income was $104,950. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the town was $45,447. About 8.6% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 9.9% of those under age 18 and 8.2% of those age 65 or over.

In 2000, 46.7% of Johnston residents identified themselves as being of [Italian](/wiki/Italy "Italy") heritage. This was the highest percentage of [Italian Americans](/wiki/Italian_American "Italian American") of any municipality in the country.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Parks and recreation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnston,_Rhode_Island&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Parks and recreation")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cc/Johnston_War_Memorial_Park_footbridge_and_sign.jpg/250px-Johnston_War_Memorial_Park_footbridge_and_sign.jpg)](/wiki/File:Johnston_War_Memorial_Park_footbridge_and_sign.jpg)

Johnston War Memorial Park

Local parks include the Johnston War Memorial Park and [Snake Den State Park](/wiki/Snake_Den_State_Park "Snake Den State Park"). Former Providence Mayor Joe Paolino purchased the *Columbus* statue that had been removed from [Columbus Square](/wiki/Columbus_Square_(Providence) "Columbus Square (Providence)") in Providence.[[11]](#cite_note-11) In 2023, the statue was donated to the city and placed in Memorial Park with the unveiling held on [Columbus Day](/wiki/Columbus_Day "Columbus Day").[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnston,_Rhode_Island&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Government")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2c/Johnston_Municipal_Court%2C_Rhode_Island.jpg/250px-Johnston_Municipal_Court%2C_Rhode_Island.jpg)](/wiki/File:Johnston_Municipal_Court,_Rhode_Island.jpg)

Johnston Municipal Court

The town is governed by a mayor (currently [Joe Polisena Jr.](/wiki/Joe_Polisena_Jr. "Joe Polisena Jr.")) and a five-member town council.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Johnston is policed by the [Johnston Police Department](/wiki/Johnston_Police_Department_(Rhode_Island) "Johnston Police Department (Rhode Island)").

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnston,_Rhode_Island&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Education")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0c/Mohr_Memorial_Library%2C_Johnston_Rhode_Island.jpg/250px-Mohr_Memorial_Library%2C_Johnston_Rhode_Island.jpg)](/wiki/File:Mohr_Memorial_Library,_Johnston_Rhode_Island.jpg)

Mohr Memorial Library

The Johnston Public School System has four elementary schools, one middle school, and one high school. [Johnston Senior High School](/wiki/Johnston_Senior_High_School "Johnston Senior High School") is a 2005 [Rhode Island Department of Education](/wiki/Rhode_Island_Department_of_Education "Rhode Island Department of Education") Regents' Commended School.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

In 2008, the Johnston School Committee decided to close both Graniteville and Calef Elementary schools. Students affected by the closures were transferred to Brown Avenue Elementary School and Winsor Hill Elementary School. This decision was not without controversy, as school officials, parents and teachers complained of inadequate staffing, increased neighborhood traffic and lack of attention for special-needs students.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnston,_Rhode_Island&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Media")]

Johnston has one local weekly newspaper, the *Johnston Sun Rise.*[[17]](#cite_note-17) The paper is complimentary, and can be found in many Johnston businesses.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnston,_Rhode_Island&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Economy")]

Insurance company [FM Global](/wiki/FM_Global "FM Global") is based in Johnston.

In 2018, Providence-based [Citizens Bank](/wiki/Citizens_Financial_Group "Citizens Financial Group") opened a $285 million corporate campus in the town. The project encompasses 425,000 square feet and employs around 3,000 people.[[18]](#cite_note-18)
